build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: Each floor is a grid-based encounter where the player plants seeds on soil tiles. Seeds grow over turns (sprout -> mature -> active) and provide effects when mature. Planting costs energy. Score 0 if no planting mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if seeds can be placed but do not grow or cost no resource. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Mature plants provide distinct effects: attack plants damage adjacent enemies, healing plants restore gardener health, wall plants block enemy paths, and energy plants generate resources. At least 4 plant effect types exist. Score 0 if plants have no effect. Score 0.5 at most if fewer than 3 effect types exist. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Enemies approach the gardener from the opposite side of the grid. If they reach the gardener, they deal damage. The player must use plants strategically to defeat or block enemies before they arrive. Score 0 if no enemies or threat exists. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but plants cannot affect them. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The player carries a seed deck and draws seeds each turn. Between floors, new seeds can be drafted and unwanted seeds composted (removed). Deck management shapes strategy. Score 0 if no deck or seed management exists. Score 0.5 at most if seeds exist but cannot be added or removed between floors. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Seasons rotate every few floors (spring, summer, autumn, winter), changing which plants thrive. Spring boosts growth speed, summer strengthens attacks, autumn yields bonus resources, winter slows growth. The current season is visible. Score 0 if no season mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if seasons change visually but have no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 8 distinct seed/plant types exist with different growth times, effects, and seasonal bonuses. Plants are visually distinct at each growth stage. Score 0 if fewer than 4 plant types exist. Score 0.5 at most if plants exist but are not visually distinct. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 4 distinct enemy types with different speeds, health, and abilities (fast runners, armored tanks, flyers that ignore walls, spawners). Different enemies require different plant strategies. Score 0 if fewer than 3 enemy types exist. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but all behave identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A draft screen between floors offers 3 new seed choices. A compost option removes seeds from the deck. Seed rarity increases on deeper floors. Score 0 if no drafting exists. Score 0.5 at most if drafting exists but offers no meaningful choice. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A greenhouse meta-layer persists between runs — seeds planted there provide small starting bonuses for future runs. The greenhouse state saves between sessions. Score 0 if no meta-progression exists. Score 0.5 at most if a greenhouse exists but provides no gameplay benefit. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A results screen shows floors cleared, plants grown, enemies defeated, seeds collected, and seasons experienced. A boss floor exists as the final challenge. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows a garden growing over dungeon stones with the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> floor encounter -> draft screen -> season change -> next floor without restarting. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During encounters, the grid clearly shows soil tiles, planted seeds with growth stage, mature plant effects, enemy positions, gardener health, energy, and current season. Score 0 if no gameplay display exists. Score 0.5 at most if displays are incomplete. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: plants at different growth stages, enemies, soil tiles, and effects are all clearly distinguishable. Score 0 if elements blend together. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, grid, plants, enemies, seasons, and UI — a consistent garden-dungeon aesthetic with natural palette that shifts with seasons. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Plants have distinct illustrated sprites at each growth stage. Enemies have character art. The grid terrain changes visually with seasons. Score 0 if plants or enemies are colored squares. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: energy bar, health display, seed hand, draft screen, season indicator, and greenhouse use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: seeds sprout with growth animation, mature plants pulse when activating, enemies have walk cycles, season transitions have visual effects, and combat produces impact particles. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
